data.title: Strathcona Resources makes C$5.9B takeover offer for MEG Energy
data.description:
Strathcona Resources (OTCPK:STHRF) said late Thursday it plans to launch a takeover bid for Canadian oil and gas producing peer MEG Energy (OTCPK:MEGEF) that values the company at ~C$5.9B (US$4.25B), in a deal that would create the country's fifth-largest oil producer.

Strathcona (OTCPK:STHRF) offered to buy all MEG Energy's (OTCPK:MEGEF) issued and outstanding common stock for 0.62/share and C$4.10/share in cash, which it said represents a total consideration of C$23.27 per MEG share, a premium of 9.3% to MEG Energy's closing price on May 15.

Strathcona (OTCPK:STHRF) said it made a formal takeover proposal to MEG Energy's (OTCPK:MEGEF) board on April 28, which was rejected on May 13; the company said it believes the benefits of a combination are significant enough that MEG shareholders should be able to decide for themselves.

data.title: Land Securities Non-GAAP EPRA EPS of $50.30, net rental income of $552M
data.description:
• Land Securities press release (OTCPK:LSGOF): FY Non-GAAP EPRA EPS of 50.30p.
  • Net rental income of £552M (+0.4% Y/Y).
  • Group LTV of 38.4% and average net debt/EBITDA of 7.7x pro-forma for disposals since year-end, as long 9.6-year average debt maturity underpins resilience of capital base.
  • Further LFL growth and efficiency improvements, alongside portfolio rebalancing to enhance long term growth, provides c. 20% EPRA EPS growth potential by FY30, with c. 2-4% growth expected in FY26.

data.title: Dividend Roundup: Chevron, BlackRock, 3M, Alcoa, and more
data.description:
This week's dividend activity included increased payouts from Chubb (CB) and Global Ship Lease (GSL) as well as declarations from companies such as BlackRock (NYSE:BLK) and 3M (NYSE:MMM).

Additionally, looking towards next week, industry players such as Chevron (NYSE:CVX) and Alcoa (NYSE:AA) will see the ex-dividend dates for their upcoming dividend payments.

See a breakdown below of some of the highlights of this week, along with some upcoming activity on the dividend front.

Dividend Increases

Here are some companies that have announced a dividend increase in the past week:

Global Ship Lease (GSL)

  • Dividend declared: May 12
  • New dividend amount: $0.525
  • Previous amount: $0.45
  • Increase: 16.7%

Curtis-Wright (CW)

  • Dividend declared: May 14
  • New dividend amount: $0.24
  • Previous amount: $0.21
  • Increase: 14.3%

Chubb (CB)

  • Dividend declared: May 15
  • New dividend amount: $0.97
  • Previous amount: $0.91
  • Increase: 6.6%

Dividend Declarations

Here are some companies that have announced a dividend in the past week:

Waste Management (WM)

  • Dividend declared: May 12
  • Dividend amount: $0.825

3M (NYSE:MMM)

  • Dividend declared: May 13
  • Dividend amount: $0.73

Kohl's (KSS)

  • Dividend declared: May 14
  • Dividend amount: $0.125

BlackRock (NYSE:BLK)

  • Dividend declared: May 15
  • Dividend amount: $5.21

Upcoming Dividend Calendar

Here are some companies that have an ex-dividend date coming next week:

Chevron (NYSE:CVX)

  • Ex-dividend date: May 19
  • Payout date: June 10

Alcoa (NYSE:AA)

  • Ex-dividend date: May 20
  • Payout date: June 6

Hasbro (HAS)

  • Ex-dividend date: May 21
  • Payout date: June 4

Discover (DFS)

  • Ex-dividend date: May 13
  • Payout date: June 2

data.title: Charter stock gains on merger deal with rival Cox Communications
data.description:
Charter (NASDAQ:CHTR) to combine with Cox Communications in a cash-and-stock deal that values the rival cable provider at an enterprise value of $34.5 billion.

The deal consists of $21.9 billion of equity and $12.6 billion of net debt and other obligations, and Charter will acquire Cox Communications' commercial fiber and managed IT and cloud businesses. Cox Enterprises will contribute Cox Communications' residential cable business to Charter Holdings.

Based on Charter's share count as of March 31, Cox Enterprises will own approximately 23% of the combined entity's fully diluted shares outstanding, on an as-converted, as-exchanged basis, and pro forma for the closing of the Liberty Broadband merger.

It is expected that Charter's combination with Cox will be completed contemporaneously with the previously announced Liberty Broadband merger.

As a result, Liberty Broadband will cease to be a direct shareholder in Charter (NASDAQ:CHTR).

Charter's shares rose over 5% in premarket trading.

data.title: Regeneron wins over $400M jury award as Amgen found liable in antitrust case
data.description:
A federal jury in Delaware has awarded Regeneron (NASDAQ:REGN) more than $400M in damages after finding that Amgen (NASDAQ:AMGN) violated U.S. antitrust laws by forcing pharmacy benefit managers ((PBMs)) to select its cholesterol drug Repatha over the former’s rival drug Praluent.

The jurors in a Delaware District Court issued the verdict on Thursday, siding with REGN over claims that Amgen (NASDAQ:AMGN) unlawfully bundled its anti-inflammatory drugs Enbrel and Otezla to convince PBMs to prefer Repatha over Praluent.

While Enbrel and Otezla are not heart medicines, Amgen (NASDAQ:AMGN) allegedly threatened to deny rebate payments to PBMs unless they picked Repatha over Praluent. “Amgen prevented Regeneron from competing on a level playing field and unfairly denied patients access to Praluent,” REGN said in a press release.

Regeneron (NASDAQ:REGN) won $135.6M in compensatory damages and $271.2M in punitive damages.

Praluent, a PCSK9 inhibitor developed by Regeneron (NASDAQ:REGN) and Sanofi (SNY) under a global collaboration agreement, generated $765M in sales worldwide, including $241.7M from the U.S. in 2024, with ~20% YoY growth.

Repatha, designed to work with a similar mechanism to reduce low-density lipoprotein (LDL) cholesterol, or “bad” cholesterol, added $2.2B in sales for Amgen (AMGN) in the same year with ~36% YoY growth.

data.title: Codere Online Luxembourg reports Q1 results
data.description:
• Codere Online Luxembourg press release (NASDAQ:CDRO): Q1 Total revenue was €54.3 mm in Q1 2025, while net gaming revenue was €57.0 mm in the period, 8% above Q1 2024.
  • Net loss was €0.7 mm in Q1 2025

data.title: STAAR Surgical Company look to conduct $30M share buyback program
data.description:
• STAAR Surgical Company (NASDAQ:STAA) said its board has authorized a share repurchase program under which the company may repurchase up to $30 million of its outstanding common stock.
  • The firm is looking to finance the buybacks with cash on hand and cash generated from operations.
  • The company added that as of March 28, it had $222.8 million in cash, cash equivalents and investments available for sale and no outstanding debt.

data.title: Granite Construction says COO James Radich to retire
data.description:
• Granite Construction (NYSE:GVA) said its chief operating officer and executive vice president, James Radich, will retire from the roles. 
  • Radich will retire on July 4.

data.title: Baby is given world's first personalized CRISPR therapy
data.description:
A big breakthrough is being hailed in the world of gene editing, with the first custom-designed treatment using CRISPR technology (NASDAQ:CRSP). The therapy was developed for an infant named KJ Muloon, who was born with a rare genetic disorder called carbamoyl-phosphate synthetase 1 (CPS 1). The condition prevents liver cells from breaking down proteins properly, resulting in an ammonia buildup that can lead to irreversible brain damage and possibly even death.

Snapshot: CPS 1 is typically treated with conventional methods, like protein restriction, nitrogen scavengers, and drugs to reduce ammonia, but the treatment may only be partially effective. A liver transplant can also be a solution, but patients have to be at least a year old and a donor has to be found. As a result, an experimental alternative was pursued, which delivered the CRISPR base-editing therapy using lipid nanoparticles. 

A low dose was first given when KJ was only six months old, which was followed up by a higher intravenous infusion after careful monitoring. He just finished his third and final dose and seems to be responding well, with reduced blood ammonia concentrations and better tolerance to dietary protein. No serious adverse effects despite concurrent viral infections, and while it's still early to know the full extent of things, doctors were able to halve the medication he receives to reduce ammonia in his body.

Watershed moment? While CRISPR (NASDAQ:CRSP) is currently approved by the FDA to treat sickle-cell anemia and beta thalassemia, the treatments for those diseases rely on a one-size-fits-all approach. In contrast, KJ's treatment was custom-tailored to his genetic makeup and brings hope that "on-demand" therapies of the future could be designed to fit an individual patient's needs. The price tag for any gene editing therapy is still prohibitive (personalized therapy even more so), but the hope is that will change down the line as things move towards a platform-based approach. "As we get better at doing this, economies of scale will kick in, and you can expect the cost to come down orders of magnitude,” said lead researcher Dr. Kiran Musunuru of the University of Pennsylvania and the Children's Hospital of Philadelphia.

data.title: Los Angeles votes for minimum wage hike for tourism workers
data.description:
Los Angeles lawmakers have voted to approve a proposal that would make the city home to the highest minimum wage in the U.S. 

The Los Angeles City Council voted 12-3 to clear the plan to raise tourism workers' minimum pay to $30 per hour by 2028, when the city is slated to host the Olympic Games. The current minimum wage for workers at large hotels is over $18 an hour. 

The wage hike would apply to hotels with over 60 rooms and businesses at Los Angeles International Airport. The minimum wage would be gradually increased, starting with hourly pay of $22.50 in 2025, then $25 in 2026, $27.50 in 2027, and finally $30 in 2028.

The proposal also includes requirements for healthcare benefits as well as six hours of training to each hotel worker, covering safety, worker rights, and other topics.

The industry has pushed back against the proposal, saying it would pressure businesses that are already struggling with a slowdown in tourism.

"The council ignored dire warnings from top city officials and business leaders who stressed that this is not the time to pass an extreme ordinance that will kill jobs and hurt the tourism industry," the American Hotel and Lodging Association said after the vote.

"Instead, the city council elected to jeopardize economic recovery and stability by advancing policies that will lead to hotel closures and thousands of layoffs," it added. 

Since the vote on the proposal was not unanimous, a second vote will be held next week.
